extraction_method: Claude Opus 4.7 vision, reading three cropped views (plan + interior elevation + spec table) from page 2, unaided by the Excel ground truth
reasoning_notes: Plan view shows dimension ladder 20|280|600|400|800|20 = 2,120mm total run.

Interior elevation shows upper dimension ladder 20|260|20|600|20|380|800|20 = 2,120mm — upper segment widths don't match plan (upper has 260 where plan has 280), suggesting plan includes base-cabinet widths while upper shows the wall-cabinet-to-filler segmentation.
The 600mm upper segment has the '디럭스형후드' (deluxe hood) label, so no wall cabinet there — it's hood space.
The 5× 400mm labels visible in the elevation middle are tile-grid reference dimensions (400×250 ceramic tile layout), NOT cabinet widths.
The X-boxed area on plan right side is the refrigerator position — no base cabinet there, but wall cabinet continues above.
Upper cabinets: 800H; lower cabinets: 700H; backsplash: 580H; plinth: 150H. Cabinet depths: 290D upper, 570D lower.
Wall-cabinet end panels: visible as thin vertical strips between upper cabinets — size guessed at 20D filler but unable to confirm dimension without side-view detail.
Trim pieces (baseboard, valance/fascia) inferred from spec table and standard LH detail but NOT directly measurable in these cropped views.
